scenario: questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood

turns.user_message: I'm 25 and I feel like I have no idea who I am. Everyone around me seems to know what they want and I'm just... floating.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
I tried to talk about this with my friend but they just said I should just get over it and move on.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
That's a good question. Let me think... I guess I feel like I should have this figured out by now.
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
My my friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood is never going to get better.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood and I should just deal with it.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood control my life more than I realized.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood control my life more than I realized.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
I think the real issue isn't questioning personal identity and values in early adulthood — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
I think what I'm taking away from today is I'm allowed to struggle with this and that doesn't make me weak. I want to sit with that.
I want to try something different this week. Maybe being honest with one person about how I'm actually doing.
This has been really helpful. I didn't expect to go this deep today.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
